justification: All pass criteria met: three files created matching MJ's naming spec (coupon-repository.ts, coupon-service.ts, coupon-service.test.ts); CouponRepository is an interface; CouponService uses constructor injection; all 5 ACs implemented with correct error codes (COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, COUPON_ALREADY_USED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET); tests use mock repository and cover all 5 ACs with explicit test names; files_changed lists all three files with purpose; acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs to specific test names.

observations.text: All 5 ACs tested with explicit, descriptive test case names that reference the AC number
AC1 tested with specific value (cartTotal * 0.90) plus fractional total edge case
Error codes exactly match Bird's spec (COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET, COUPON_ALREADY_USED)
Mock repository via factory function — no real database calls in tests
Boundary testing included (minimumOrder at-limit, single-use unused, non-single-use with used=true)
CouponError has typed code property for programmatic error handling

Validation order listed in summary (NOT_FOUND → EXPIRED → ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET → ALREADY_USED) doesn't match expected rubric order (existence → expiry → single-use → minimum order → calculation), though spec doesn't mandate order and the deviation is documented
Deviation notes reusing existing files rather than creating fresh — suggests files pre-existed, though all content is spec-compliant

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: three files created matching MJ's naming spec (coupon-repository.ts, coupon-service.ts, coupon-service.test.ts); CouponRepository is an interface; CouponService injects the repository; all 5 ACs implemented with correct error codes (COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, COUPON_ALREADY_USED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET); tests use mock repository and cover all 5 ACs; files_changed lists all three files with purposes; acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs to specific test names.

observations.text: All three files created with clear separation of concerns matching MJ's architecture spec
CouponRepository explicitly described as a 'pure interface' — not a concrete class
All 5 error codes match Bird's spec exactly (COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET, COUPON_ALREADY_USED)
AC1 tested with specific value: cartTotal=100, expected=90, plus fractional case $55.50→$49.95
AC5 (single-use) explicitly covered — common omission avoided
Tests use lightweight mock repository with no real database calls
Boundary cases tested beyond minimum requirements (fractional totals, minimum-order boundary, unused single-use)

Validation order is not-found → expired → minimum-order → already-used, which differs from the expected order (not-found → expired → single-use → minimum-order → calculation). Functional impact is minor but deviates from spec.

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: three files created matching MJ's naming spec (coupon-repository.ts, coupon-service.ts, coupon-service.test.ts); CouponRepository is an interface; CouponService uses constructor injection; all 5 ACs implemented with correct error codes (COUPON_NOT_FOUND, COUPON_EXPIRED, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET, COUPON_ALREADY_USED); tests use in-memory mock repository; files_changed lists all three files with purposes; acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 5 ACs to test names.

observations.text: All three files created with clear separation of concerns per MJ's spec
CouponRepository explicitly defined as an interface, not a class
Constructor injection used for CouponService — testable design
Error codes match Bird's spec exactly (COUPON_EXPIRED, COUPON_NOT_FOUND, ORDER_MINIMUM_NOT_MET, COUPON_ALREADY_USED)
AC1 tested with specific values: cartTotal=100 → 90, plus fractional case 55.5 → 49.95
AC5 (single-use) not overlooked — both already-used and first-use cases tested
Boundary cases included (cart exactly at minimum order threshold)

Validation order differs from rubric's expected order (rubric: existence→expiry→single-use→minimum→calc; agent: existence→expiry→minimum→single-use→calc) — minimum-order checked before single-use. Minor deviation, all checks still present.
